text.title: Terraform HCL Syntax Reference
text.summary: Provider configuration, expressions, for_each, dynamic blocks, built-in functions, and data source patterns.

rule.statement: Configure default_tags on the AWS provider to apply Project, Environment, and ManagedBy=terraform tags to all resources automatically.
rule.rationale: Default tags eliminate manual tag repetition and ensure every resource is tagged for cost allocation without per-resource configuration.

rule.statement: Use provider aliases for multi-region or multi-account deployments; reference them with provider = aws.alias_name on specific resources.
rule.rationale: Alias providers allow a single configuration to manage resources across multiple regions or accounts without separate configurations.

rule.statement: Use for expressions to transform lists and maps; use dynamic blocks for repeated nested blocks driven by a variable.
rule.rationale: Inline for expressions and dynamic blocks keep configurations DRY without requiring module extraction for simple transformations.

section.title: Built-in Functions

rule.statement: Use cidrsubnet for CIDR calculations, slice for AZ selection, merge for combining tags, and jsonencode/jsondecode for IAM policies.
rule.rationale: Built-in functions keep numeric and structural logic in Terraform rather than requiring external scripting or hardcoded values.

section.note: Key functions: cidrsubnet(cidr, bits, index), slice(list, start, end), merge(map1, map2), length(list), lookup(map, key, default), toset(list).

rule.statement: Add validation blocks to variables for constrained inputs (environment names, instance families); use sensitive = true for secrets.
rule.rationale: Validation surfaces misconfigurations at plan time before any API calls. sensitive = true prevents secrets from appearing in plan output and logs.

rule.statement: Use locals for the name_prefix pattern, CIDR calculations, and common_tags — compute once, reference everywhere.
rule.rationale: Locals avoid repeating the same interpolation logic across many resources and make global renames a single-line change.
